meta.role: Academic Program Administrator, Career & Professional Development, DHBB (Mann School)
meta.note: DRAFTS for Jamie's review. Adapted from her voice corpus — verify and edit before pasting. Each answer 80–150 words.

answers.question: Why are you interested in this role?
answers.answer: I'm drawn to this role because it sits right at the intersection of two things I love—running programs that genuinely help people, and doing it with a research-driven mindset. As a Program Enablement Manager, coordinating workshops, managing vendor and event logistics, and translating stakeholder needs into well-designed programming are already core parts of my day-to-day. What excites me here is the chance to do that work in service of students and alumni building their careers. With a master's in Applied Organizational Psychology, I treat programs as systems to be studied: I diagnose the real gap with data, design a solution with the people involved, then measure whether it worked. That's exactly the approach a strong career and professional-development function needs, and it's the impact I hope to bring to USC Mann.

answers.question: Why USC?
answers.answer: USC is where I earned my MS in Applied Organizational Psychology, so this one is personal. My time at USC shaped how I think about people, programs, and evidence—and I experienced firsthand how much a thoughtful, well-run academic and career-development team shapes a student's trajectory. I'd be honored to give back to that same community, this time from the other side of the desk. Beyond the personal connection, I admire how USC Mann treats career and professional development as a structured, high-touch program rather than an afterthought—building real employer relationships, dedicated internship pipelines, and data-informed program reviews. That systems-minded, student-first approach mirrors how I work, and it's the kind of place where I know I can contribute meaningfully. As a fellow Trojan, I'd love to help current students find their footing.

answers.question: Describe your experience managing programs or coordinating professional-development initiatives.
answers.answer: In my current role as a Program Enablement Manager, I coordinate logistics for 20+ educational programs and 10+ vendors—owning schedules, materials, and delivery across a distributed team. I conduct needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders to design programming that fits the people it serves, and I facilitate live webinars and information sessions for 600+ participants, contributing to a 78% enrollment rate. I also developed guides and workflows for 70+ cross-functional staff, reducing onboarding time by 75%. Earlier, at NextGen Healthcare in Los Angeles, I administered and analyzed 2,000+ survey responses and organized the findings into reports for leadership—directly relevant to running alumni and internship surveys for program reviews. My approach is consistent: diagnose the gap before designing, build in feedback loops, and measure behavior, not just attendance, so programs keep improving over time.
